events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need decide compliance. Scenario: spring can travel pins not pulled after hydro. Hangers & supports. Spring hangers have travel pins (locking pins) that must be removed after hydrostatic test to allow spring to function. If not pulled, spring support is locked, not providing support, pipe load may not be supported properly, stress, potential failure. At rough-complete stage, after hydro. This is non-compliant and installed-defective. Decision fail. Risk high? Could cause pipe support failure, spring damage, excessive stress. Commercial project. Need include references: MSS SP-58, project support standards, manufacturer instructions. Also maybe mention IBC/ASME B31.1? But references on hand are project support standards, MSS SP-58. Confidence high. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 85. component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Findings: spring hanger travel pins remain engaged after hydrostatic test; spring cannot deflect/support design load. Actions: remove travel pins, verify cold/hot settings, inspect all spring supports, document. Need JSON only. Ensure no prose outside.

  "findings": [
    "Spring hanger travel pins were not removed after hydrostatic testing.",
    "The spring support is locked and cannot deflect to support the design pipe load.",
    "Condition violates manufacturer installation requirements and project support standards."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove all spring hanger travel pins that were left engaged after hydro.",
    "Verify spring cold/hot settings and load indicators per manufacturer data.",
    "Inspect every spring support for proper operation and positioning.",
    "Document corrective actions and re-inspect before close-out."
  ],
  "rationale": "Spring hanger travel pins are temporary locks used during shipping and hydrostatic testing; they must be removed after hydro so the spring can absorb pipe movement and support the intended load. Leaving them in place renders the support non-functional, creates a pipe stress/support deficiency, and is non-compliant with MSS SP-58 and project support standards.",
  "references": [
    "MSS SP-58 - Pipe Hangers and Supports - Materials, Design, Manufacture, Selection, Application, and Installation",
    "Project support standards",
    "Spring hanger manufacturer installation instructions"
  ]
}
